title: rubus × semipubescens
intro: Rubus × semipubescens, also known as the black raspberry, is a hybrid plant that is a cross between Rubus occidentalis and Rubus idaeus, and is characterized by its dark purple to black fruit. The plant's leaves and roots have been found to possess medicinal properties, with compounds like anthocyanins and ellagic acid showing potential in reducing inflammation and fighting cancer cells. The health benefits of Rubus × semipubescens include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. Rubus × semipubescens is often prepared as an herbal tea, juice, or supplement, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other food products due to its nutritional value and flavor.
